title: Two locals, including teacher, held for ‘providing logistic support’ to Saifullah terror group in Kishtwar
content: The police said these locals “extended support to the Saifullah group”, comprising around six non-local terrorists who were neutralised in multiple operations in the Kishtwar district in the past one year

title: ATS questions 57 in Maharashtra over alleged gangster network links
content: Intelligence inputs received over a period of time indicated that gangsters operating from across the border were using social media to recruit youths from Maharashtra, say officials

title: Nicobarese oppose proposal for three wildlife sanctuaries
content: Tribal Council says Centre did not consult residents of Little Nicobar, Meroe, and Menchal islands, where the sanctuaries are being planned

title: West Bengal CM gives sanction to CBI to prosecute officials in several scams
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced that the policy of the previous government relating to a ban on the sale of potatoes to other states stands lifted with immediate effect

title: The ‘cinematisation’ of politics
content: The youth don’t necessarily crave for a change — what they crave for is a narrative; some purpose that empowers them with direction

title: Annakili@50: The extraordinary journey of maestro Ilaiyaraaja
content: If there had been no Panchu Arunachalam, there would have been no Ilaiyaraaja or Gangai Amaran. He was our first god. He lit the light in our lives, says Gangai Amaran, younger brother of Ilaiyaraaja, who was the rhythm guitarist for the film

title: BJP, Congress attack SP over party spokesperson’s remark on Brahmins; FIR registered
content: Bhati offers unconditional apology, claims certain parts of his speech were selectively edited to spread ‘malicious propaganda’

title: GCDA contests government order allotting land adjoining Kaloor stadium to college
content: Agency says Revenue dept. lacked authority to allot its land to college without consulting it; college dismisses GCDA’s contention as baseless, citing a govt. order establishing its ownership of the land

title: 24x7 control rooms for disaster management to be set up in Ernakulam
content: The centres will be set up as part of the district’s monsoon preparedness measures to handle rain-related incidents. The monsoon is expected to reach the Kerala coast early this year, by May 24

title: Vijay Narayan earns rare distinction of being Advocate General under two different governments
content: The government led by TVK appoints him as the top most law officer of the State; he had held the office under the AIADMK government too between 2017 and 2021

title: Congress unveils ‘Bhu Guarantee’
content: The announcements include a reduction in the guidance value cost that needs to be paid for conversion from B-Khata to A-Khata, and regularisation of buildings in BDA areas constructed before 2008

title: Ilaiyaraaja’s trendsetter movie Annakili turns 50
content: A black-and-white film running successfully for 200 days was remarkable, and much of that achievement was due to the songs composed by Ilaiyaraaja, says actor Sivakumar

title: Bengaluru: Nearly 80% land acquisition done for PRR Package 1, work order to be issued in June
content: This will be the first on-ground work to begin for PRR, which has been delayed for over two decades.

title: Iran war shows military force alone cannot be solution to conflicts: T.S. Tirumurti
content: The former Ambassador and Permanent Representative of India to the United Nations was speaking at the inauguration of Diplomacy and Sustainability Dialogues 2026

title: India should have stepped into vacuum left by U.S. as guarantor of security in West Asia: Talmiz Ahmad
content: Speaking on the topic ‘India’s Diplomatic Balancing in West Asia: Strategic Autonomy, Energy Security and Global Responsibility’, the former Ambassador remarked that “India is not a serious role-player as far as West Asia and the crisis over the last two and a half years is concerned.”

title: Bengal CM Adhikari retains Bhabanipur seat, says wont let people of Nandigram feel his absence
content: CM Suvendu Adhikari joined an “Abhinandan Yatra” in Bhabanipur with other BJP leaders, stating it was a procession to thank people of the constituency, as directed by State BJP president Samik Bhattacharya

title: Tamil Nadu jewellers to stop sale of gold coins
content: Association says the measure aims to help reduce gold imports, asks government to immediately ban ETFs, digital gold sales and restrict gold importers, channelling agents and bullion dealers

title: Learn from Sri Lanka’s experience on impact of fertilizer supply chains: experts
content: The experts participating in a panel discussion on the topic ‘Impact of West Asia Crisis on Economic Disruptions and Sustainability: Implications for India’ say India should continue to follow the current policies while focussing on a transition to sustainable practices in the long term

title: CBSE Class 12 results: Tamil Nadu registers 95.25% pass; girls outperform boys
content: As many as 46,901 boys and 41,245 girls from Tamil Nadu wrote the CBSE Class 12 exams, wherein girls outperformed boys (94%), with a pass rate of 96.68%

title: TDB signs pact with KITSFRA for complete digitisation of temples, offices
content: Temple administration, file movement in offices, financial transactions in temples and offices, services for devotees and various online facilities connected to the Sabarimala temple will be brought under a digital platform

title: Telangana govt. advises people to take adequate precautions in view of scorching heat
content: Revenue Minister asks district collectors to take necessary measures to avoid loss of life or property

title: Device with low-grade explosives found inside private hospital in Pune; ATS joins probe
content: A team of the Bomb Detection and Disposal Squad was immediately rushed to the spot and it took the object to a nearby ground and disposed of it

title: Iran working on Hormuz ‘protocol’ to cover ‘costs’, says Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi
content: Hinting at UAE, Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi says “a neighbouring country that is also a member of the BRICS” is trying to scuttle a joint statement by seeking condemnation of Iran

title: Mehbooba welcomes RSS leader’s ‘support for talks with Pakistan’
content: We welcome the RSS general secretary’s statement; this vindicates the PDP’s stand and that of its founder Mufti Muhammad Sayeed, who always advocated for dialogue with Pakistan, she said

title: Police register FIR after viral video shows BJP worker’s kin firing into the air at wedding
content: The video sparked outrage online and a backlash from the Opposition, the local DD Nagar Police then registered an FIR

title: Lawrence Bishnoi gang stakes claim on gunfire outside Diljit Dosanjh's manager in Karnal
content: Punjab Finance Minister Harpal Singh Cheema alleged that a few days ago, the BJP had asked Diljit Dosanjh to join the party, but he refused

title: A.P. CM Naidu cuts convoy size, tells VVIPs to adopt austerity measures
content: Naidu insists that 50 per cent reduction in convoy vehicles be implemented across all levels of government administration and among public representatives

title: Local transport, storage and human handling are the weakest links in conducting NEET, according to panel report
content: K. Radhakrishnan Committee had sought overhaul of National Testing Agency; it explained that while doing away with third-party involvement in the exam process is recommended, a district-wise assessment of infrastructure — especially in rural areas — is equally important

title: Around 83% of candidates lost their deposit in Tamil Nadu polls, says ECI
content: In West Bengal polls, 78% lost their security amount; the least number (63%) of candidates lost their deposit was in Assam

title: Modi preaches austerity while denying dignity to the poor, splurging on self-promotion: Kharge
content: Congress chief says Centre has not raised old-age pensions for 12 years despite soaring inflation almost halving purchasing power; says BJP government spent almost ₹6,000 cr on publicity during decade in power

title: CBSE Class 12 results record dip in pass percentage, girls outperform boys again
content: The national pass percentage stands at 85.20%, a drop of 3.19% from last year’s 88.39%; Trivandrum leads the charts with a staggering 95.62% pass rate, followed closely by Chennai and Bengaluru

title: Congress takes a swipe at RSS leader over Pakistan remarks
content: Hosabale had said there should ‘always be a window’ for dialogue with Pakistan and stressed the importance of people-to-people engagement in easing tensions between the two countries

title: How the anti-defection law is going to operate in the AIADMK case?
content: There are two grounds for disqualification in the event of defection — when a member voluntarily gives up membership of his or her political party and when a member votes or abstains from voting contrary to any direction issued by his or her political party

title: India weighs joint Centre-State funding framework for heatwave mitigation
content: The shift follows years in which heatwave responses sat outside the main disaster financing architecture

title: Don't be ‘self-centred’, RTE is for the benefit of children: Supreme Court tells teachers, States in TET review
content: “Higher educational qualifications are sought to get better teachers; unless a child gets a good quality education, how will she improve?” Justice Manmohan asks petitioners

title: All religious practices presumed protected unless they violate public order, health, morality: Centre in Sabarimala review hearing
content: Centre argues that “essential religious practices” absent in the Constitutional text, is only part of SC  interpretation; says individual freedom of conscience (Article 25) linked to denominational rights (Article 26)

title: PM cuts size of convoy after fuel conservation appeal
content: Following the appeal, U.P. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath directed a 50% reduction in the escort vehicles for himself and his State Cabinet colleagues

title: Palaniswami sacks 26 district secretaries in the AIADMK
content: The district secretaries who were removed, included Natham R. Viswanathan, S.P. Velumani, C.Ve. Shanmugam, R. Kamaraj and C. Vijayabaskar. Of the ousted functionaries, 12 are currently the legislators of the party

title: Centre increases MSP for kharif crops, farm groups say rise inadequate amid rising input costs
content: While Centre says MSP levels have been increased significantly compared to those in 2013-14, farmer organisations criticise new rates, saying they do not factor in possible “disastrous impact” of India-United States trade Deal and other free trade agreements on agriculture sector

title: NEET paper leak case: CBI arrests five, conducts searches across the country
content: Incriminating material and electronic devices seized, following leads through “extensive technical and forensic analysis”, says CBI; two more suspects detained in Maharashtra, handed over to CBI

title: NIA brings back ‘narco-terror’ accused from Portugal
content: The National Investigation Agency has arrested an alleged “narco-terror” accused after securing his extradition from Portugal. The agency says he played a key role in a terror-financing network linked to Hizb-ul-Mujahideen, involving drug trafficking and hawala channels. Here’s what we know.

title: Iran war could become a trillion-dollar burden for US
content: Washington's campaign against Iran has surged to nearly $29 billion, surpassing initial Pentagon estimates. Experts warn the escalating costs, driven by operational and equipment replacement, could ultimately reach a staggering $1 trillion.  This financial burden is becoming a political flashpoint ahead of US elections, with concerns over inflation.

title: ‘Accept with grin’: Sanctioned by US, China sat firm turns blacklist into hiring pitch
content: Chinese satellite firm MizarVision, sanctioned by the US for allegedly supplying imagery of American military activity during the Iran conflict, has ingeniously turned the sanctions into a recruitment drive. The company posted a job advertisement featuring the US Treasury notice, inviting applicants who thrive under pressure and embrace strength.

title: 'Chase-master' Kohli returns to form with century as RCB gun down 193 vs KKR
content: Kohli reached his fifty off 32 balls and shared a key 92-run partnership with Devdutt Padikkal, who made 39 off 27 balls with seven fours. Kohli later brought up his century with a single off Arora, registering his ninth IPL hundred, the most by any batter in the tournament’s history.

title: Trump's 'CEO crew' to Beijing is almost as rich as world's third-largest economy
content: Donald Trump has landed in Beijing with 17 American companies in tow,   worth a combined $16.47 trillion—roughly 90% of China's entire 2025 GDP. Jensen Huang joined at the last minute, boarding Air Force One in Anchorage. Nvidia and Apple alone account for $9.7 trillion of that, while Meta sent its policy chief after Beijing blocked its $2 billion Manus deal.

title: Empty offices, locked buildings & fake work location: US agency flags OPT visa fraud
content: US immigration agencies have uncovered widespread fraud within the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program, involving thousands of foreign students and suspect employers. Investigations revealed fake offices, empty buildings, and companies claiming to employ hundreds with little to no actual business activity, suggesting a "guestworker pipeline" issue.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sold their electrical co for $1.7bn and gave 540 workers $240 m of it
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Type 2 diabetes: Symptoms, causes, and what science has to say
content: India grapples with a significant diabetes and prediabetes surge. Millions are affected, many unaware of their condition. High blood sugar silently harms the body, impacting vital organs. Early detection and lifestyle changes are crucial. Doctors emphasize prevention through diet, exercise, and sleep. Timely treatment is vital for managing this widespread health challenge.

title: Taylor Swift, Emily Ratajkowski and Dua Lipa’s ex share one chaotic dinner table
content: Top celebrities gathered for Lena Dunham's birthday dinner in New York. Taylor Swift, Emily Ratajkowski, and Andrew Rannells were among the guests. The event highlighted the close-knit nature of the entertainment industry. Connections between the attendees, including past relationships and professional collaborations, were noted. This gathering showcased a powerful network of influential figures.

title: Gabrielle Union opens up on finding peace and joy beyond Hollywood fame
content: Dwyane Wade and his wife, Gabrielle Union, have inspired billions of fans with the love and commitment they have shown towards each other, over the last decade. While the high profile couple live pretty lavish lives, both Dwyane Wade and Gabrielle Union remain pretty private about it.

title: Brandon Clarke’s net worth at death: Inside his $64M career earnings
content: Brandon Clarke, who tragically passed away at 29, amassed a significant fortune of $64 million in career NBA earnings. Drafted 21st overall, he secured a substantial $50 million fully guaranteed extension with the Memphis Grizzlies, demonstrating financial security solely through his basketball career. His wealth was built on contracts and modest endorsements, highlighting a life dedicated to playing the game.

title: IPL 2026: Big blow for Chennai Super Kings! Jamie Overton heads home
content: Chennai Super Kings face another blow as all-rounder Jamie Overton returns to the UK for a thigh injury assessment.  The England international was a key performer with 14 wickets and 136 runs. This latest setback adds to CSK's growing injury list, impacting their playoff aspirations despite their resilience.  The team continues to battle an ongoing crisis.

title: K'taka govt withdraws hijab ban order in educational institutes, permits religious symbols
content: Karnataka has withdrawn the previous BJP government's order banning hijabs in educational institutions, now permitting limited traditional and faith-based symbols like hijabs, turbans, and sacred threads. The new guidelines aim to balance discipline with constitutional values of equality and inclusion, ensuring no student is denied access for wearing permitted symbols.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for May 14, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 14, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: ‘It was truly heartbreaking’: Rashid, Nabi visit hospitalised Shapoor Zadran
content: Afghanistan's cricket stars Rashid Khan and Mohammad Nabi visited former teammate Shapoor Zadran in a Greater Noida hospital. Zadran is battling a rare, life-threatening immune disorder. Nabi shared a heartfelt update, expressing his pain at seeing the once-feared bowler hospitalized and praying for his swift recovery and return to loved ones.

title: Patience as power: How Beijing turned America's Iran war into a Taiwan strategy
content: While the US and Israel engaged in conflict with Iran, China quietly leveraged the situation to expand its influence. By positioning itself as a diplomatic alternative and stabilizing energy supplies, Beijing benefited from a weakened American strategic position and growing uncertainty about US reliability among allies.

title: Virat Kohli’s animated celebration after first run vs KKR goes viral — Watch
content: Virat Kohli's first run against KKR sparked an animated celebration, a moment of immense relief after two consecutive ducks. This emotional display resonated widely, overshadowing his milestone of becoming the most-capped IPL player ever.  Kohli's single, a significant personal victory, captured the attention of fans and social media alike, highlighting his passion on the field.

title: Virat Kohli scripts IPL history, overtakes MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma for a big milestone
content: Virat Kohli has etched his name in IPL history on Wednesday, becoming the most-capped player with his 279th appearance for Royal Challengers Bengaluru. This significant milestone places him ahead of legends like MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma. Kohli's enduring presence underscores his pivotal role in the league's evolution since its inception.

title: James McAvoy quote: Failed relationships taught me not to rush love
content: James McAvoy, known for his versatile acting roles, shared a profound life lesson learned from past relationships. He emphasized the importance of observing patterns over time rather than rushing into romance based solely on initial attraction. This wisdom, gained through experience, guides his approach to love and commitment.

title: Parag Tyagi breaks silence on rumours around Shefali Jariwala’s death
content: Parag Tyagi has once again reacted to the rumours surrounding wife Shefali Jariwala’s death on June 27, 2025, and dismissed several bizarre claims circulating on social media. In a recent conversation, Parag clarified that Shefali never took injections for dieting, fairness or anti-ageing purposes and urged people to stop believing “WhatsApp University.”

title: Oops!... Pakistan did it again: The 'deny, deflect, get caught' pattern repeats
content: Pakistan's history of 'oops' moments, from allegedly harboring Iranian aircraft to denying terrorist camps, has eroded its credibility on the global stage. Decades of strategic ambiguity and carefully calibrated deniability have created a trust deficit, making its diplomatic assurances met with skepticism and every crisis a potential international embarrassment.

title: IPL 2026: Record alert! Bhuvneshwar Kumar achieves huge milestone vs KKR
content: Bhuvneshwar Kumar has equalled Yuzvendra Chahal's record for the most wickets against Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL history, claiming his 33rd scalp. The veteran seamer achieved this milestone with a crucial powerplay wicket, continuing his impressive form for Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Despite KKR posting a strong total, Bhuvneshwar's achievement marked a significant personal milestone in the tournament.

title: Connor McDavid and Auston Matthews’ combined net worth in 2026
content: Mostly through record deals, lucrative endorsement contracts and a big leap in career earnings. The captains of the Edmonton Oilers and Toronto Maple Leafs are estimated to be worth between $60 million and $100 million combined. Their commercial value has grown; their signing bonuses and worldwide popularity have also helped to boost them into hockey’s pantheon of marketing stars.

title: Delhi Police arrests influencer 'The Skin Doctor' over posts on Kapur family
content: Delhi Police arrested social media influencer Dr Neelam Singh alias The Skin Doctor. She allegedly posted tweets targeting the Kapur family. This followed the death of businessman Sunjay Kapur in London. His mother had requested a criminal investigation into the circumstances of his passing. The police are continuing their investigation into the matter.

title: Can Vande Bharat sleeper trains be a game-changer for Indian Railways?
content: Over 250 new Vande Bharat sleeper trains are in the pipeline for rollout over the next few years - all with the aim to revamp long-distance rail travel and offer a world-class service experience to passengers.

title: 'We’ve missed his presence': MI star bowler on Hardik Pandya’s absence
content: Mumbai Indians all-rounder Corbin Bosch acknowledged the significant impact of captain Hardik Pandya's absence on their disappointing IPL season. Bosch stated that the team has missed Pandya's leadership and on-field contributions, contributing to their struggles in crucial moments. The side has failed to deliver complete performances, with key moments consistently going against them.

title: 'It is right thing': Ex-Army chief backs RSS gen secy Hosabale on dialogue with Pak
content: Former Army chief General (retired) Manoj Mukund Naravane has endorsed Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale's call for increased people-to-people contact between India and Pakistan. Naravane believes stronger citizen ties can improve bilateral relations between the two countries, stating common people share similar concerns and have no stake in politics. Hosabale is the RSS number two after Sangh chief Mohan Bhagwat.

title: ‘We did not fire Sreejesh’: Hockey India responds after legend's explosive allegation
content: Hockey India clarified that legendary goalkeeper PR Sreejesh's tenure as junior men's coach concluded as per contract, denying claims of him being "fired." The federation stated his position was advertised and a new coach selected on merit. An alternative development role was offered but declined. Hockey India emphasised its commitment to developing Indian coaches.

title: 'AIADMK has been declining': CV Shanmugam targets EPS after removal from party posts
content: AIADMK leader CV Shanmugam criticized party chief Edappadi K Palaniswami, alleging continuous electoral setbacks since he assumed leadership after Jayalalithaa's death. Shanmugam highlighted that while the party faced defeats before, Jayalalithaa had the capability to bring it back to power, a feat he claims EPS has failed to achieve, leading to worsening electoral performance.

title: IPL: Why Varun Chakaravarthy is not playing in KKR’s crucial clash vs RCB
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face a significant blow as star spinner Varun Chakaravarthy is ruled out of their IPL 2026 clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru due to an injury sustained in the previous match. Uncapped pacer Saurabh Dubey makes his IPL debut as KKR navigates this crucial encounter without their key bowler, who has been instrumental in their recent resurgence.

title: Alexander Zverev denied entry at Rome restaurant before Italian Open loss
content: Alexander Zverev endured a difficult 24 hours in Rome after being denied entry to a famous restaurant and then crashing out of the Italian Open. The World No. 3 was recently turned away from the popular Japanese restaurant Zuma in central Rome because he did not have a reservation. Reports also claimed that the restaurant staff failed to recognise the German tennis star when he arrived with his brother Mischa Zverev and a friend for dinner.

title: 'Don't you understand extent of our victory?' Vijay lambasts DMK after floor test win
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's government won a trust vote with 144 MLAs supporting it. Following the victory, Vijay penned an open letter to the public, criticizing the opposition DMK for attempting to undermine his administration. He highlighted his party's electoral performance and commitment to secular values, while accusing the DMK of hypocrisy in coalition politics.

title: AIADMK crisis deepens: EPS removes rebel leaders backing CM Vijay
content: AIADMK's internal conflict escalated as party chief Palaniswami expelled the rebel leaders. These leaders had backed Tamil Nadu CM Vijay during a crucial trust vote on the assembly floor. The move follows accusations of weakening the party's anti-DMK stance and exploring alliances. This leadership struggle intensifies ahead of future elections.

title: Sanjay Gupta on Rs 300 crore ‘Dhurandhar’ gamble with Ranveer Singh
content: Filmmaker Sanjay Gupta praised Aditya Dhar's vision behind 'Dhurandhar', calling it a massive risk given Ranveer Singh's career low and Dhar's limited filmmaking experience. He credited the Ambanis' backing as a key factor in the film's success. The two-part franchise has reportedly crossed Rs 3000 crore worldwide.

title: Netanyahu's Coalition Submits Bill To Dissolve Parliament, Trigger Fresh Polls
content: The bill calls for elections "no less than 90 days" after it is approved, giving Netanyahu flexibility to choose a date when he feels he has the best chance of success.

title: US Senate Confirms Trump Nominee Kevin Warsh As Federal Reserve Chair
content: The Senate voted 54 to 45 to confirm Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, who like Trump has advocated for lower interest rates.

title: Matthew Perry Drug middleman Jailed For Two Years
content: A middleman who helped provide "Friends" actor Matthew Perry with drugs in the weeks before he died from a ketamine overdose was jailed on Wednesday in California.

title: Influencer Arrested For Social Media Posts Against Sunjay Kapur's Family
content: The police action came after a complaint was lodged in connection with the social media posts at Delhi's Vasant Kunj police station on behalf of the Kapur family.

title: German Paediatrician Charged With 130 Cases Of Child Sex Abuse Over 12 Years
content: The unidentified suspect who worked in clinics in Brandenburg state near Berlin allegedly committed the crimes over 12 years, they said in a statement, without specifying how many children were abused.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test Highlights: EPS Sacks CV Shanmugam From AIADMK Post For Voting In Vijay's Favour
content: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: A rebel faction of the AIADMK, comprising 30 MLAs, said they will support Vijay in Wednesday's floor test.

title: Karnataka Revokes 2022 Order, Allows Hijab, Sacred Thread In Schools
content: The fresh order formally revokes the earlier directive that had required strict adherence to uniforms without religious symbols, a move that had sparked a political flashpoint centred on the wearing of the hijab.

title: Live Updates: Kerala Chief Minister Announcement Tomorrow, Says Congress
content: Kerala Chief Minister Live Updates: A formal announcement is expected today, once Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge returns to Delhi and holds further talks with senior figures, including Sonia Gandhi.

title: No Work-From-Home Mandate Yet, PM Modi's Appeal Not 'Austerity': Sources
content: The clarification comes after speculation that the government could push companies towards large-scale remote working amid global uncertainty and rising concerns over fuel prices.

title: Netanyahu Visited UAE "Secretly" During War With Iran, PM's Office Says
content: "During Operation 'Lion's Roar', Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu paid a secret visit to the United Arab Emirates, where he met with UAE President Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan," his office said in a statement.

title: Jammu And Kashmir Teacher Arrested For Aiding Jaish Terrorists
content: Police called it the first major detention in the post-encounter probe into terror networks in Chatroo.

title: "Reserve Right To Respond": Araghchi Accuses Kuwait Of Attacking Iranian Boat
content: Kuwait's interior ministry said in a statement published by state news agency KUNA on Tuesday that four people arrested earlier this month as they attempted to enter the country by sea confessed to belonging to Iran's Revolutionary Guards.

title: BJP Dominates Local Body Elections in Haryana
content: Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini said under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the people's trust in both the Central and Haryana governments has grown.

title: Bomb With 7-Hour Timer Found Near Pune Hospital, Removed By Disposal Squad
content: Police immediately alerted the Bomb Detection and Disposal Squad (BDDS) and the fire brigade. Teams, including a dog squad, reached the spot and cordoned off the area.

title: "Timely Precautionary Measure": NITI Aayog Vice Chairman On PM's Forex Appeal
content: The call comes amid escalating tensions in the Middle East that threaten global oil supplies and have pushed energy prices higher, putting pressure on India's import bill and foreign exchange reserves.

title: When MLAs Cross-Vote: Decoding Vijay's Trust Motion In Tamil Nadu
content: In most cases, in case of a difference of opinion within the party, a whip is issued that would compel the rebels to toe the party line. Else, their only option is abstention or resignation, often both.

title: Amul, Mother Dairy Hike Milk Prices By Rs 2 Per Litre
content: The increase, the second by the two dairy cooperatives in 13 months, is expected to prompt similar hikes by regional dairy companies.

title: 'Not Suggesting Violence': Atishi Clarifies Arvind Kejriwal's 'Gen Z' Remark
content: Atishi alleged that NEET aspirants are subjected to stringent rules and checks when they appear for the exam, but there are no rules for those who organise it.

title: 'Costly For Government, Right For Student': Sources On Cancelling NEET Exam
content: The government chose to cancel the test involving nearly 23 lakh candidates rather than risk disadvantaging any student who prepared honestly, sources said.

title: CBI chief Praveen Sood gets second one-year extension
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: The Dragon & the Dealmaker: Tariff warrior Trump arrives in China as trade pilgrim
content: the visit comes wrapped in imperial pomp and choreographed grandeur . under the ceremony lies a more transactional reality: Trump is effectively in China hunting for a trade deal that can ease economic anxieties at home . the symbolism of the trip is striking .

title: Why 'Congress-mukt Bharat' may never become a reality
content: Why 'Congress-mukt Bharat' may not become reality Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More

title: Pokhran-II tests showed no power can bend India: Modi
content: 'Operation Shakti' was a series of five nuclear bomb explosions in may 1998 . the tests were conducted over two days in 1998: may 11 (three tests) and may 13 (two tests) modi in a post on X said: "on this very day in 1998, the nuclear tests conducted by India demonstrated to the world just how unwavering our nation's resolve is!

title: Araghchi arrives in India for BRICS meet; Iran to let more Indian ships pass Strait of Hormuz
content: foreign minister seyed abbas araghchi arrives in india . he accuses the UAE of blocking a joint declaration at the foreign ministers' meeting . "one neighbouring country" was preventing a consensus by insisting on condemnation of Iran .

title: Teacher among 2 held for aiding terrorists in J&K
content:
JAMMU: Police on Wednesday arrested two persons, including a teacher, for allegedly aiding foreign terrorists by providing them shelter and logistical support in Kishtwar district. Kishtwar SSP Naresh Singh said, “One of the two, Mashkoor Ahmed, is a teacher in the school education department.” Earlier, one Maneer Ahmed was also arrested for similar offences. Both are residents of Kishtwar.

End of Article

title: Maharashtra: Nearly 70 lakh women found ineligible under Ladki Bahin scheme after e-KYC drive
content: nearly 70 lakh beneficiaries under the state govt's flagship e-KYC have been found ineligible . the move has led to a sharp decline in the number of women receiving the monthly financial assistance of Rs 1,500 . despite repeated extensions and awareness drives, a significant number of beneficiaries failed to complete the mandatory .

title: 12th India-bound ship crosses Hormuz, one more on way
content: the ship that crossed the Strait of Hormuz has been identified as Symi, a Marshall Islands-flagged vessel, carrying LPG . the remaining one was a crude oil tanker . officials say the vessel is likely to exit the conflict-hit strait early Thursday morning .
